behaviors.description: Migrations are applied in ascending numeric order determined by the filename prefix.
behaviors.justification: BEHAVIOR-QUOTE-GATE: "applied in ascending numeric order" requires wiring discovery + ordering + execution handoff, not just a pure sort helper. MOCK-MISS: failure can occur at module boundary between file discovery and runner execution list. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess requirement beyond local filesystem fixture. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no, (b) no, (c) no, (d) no. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System because behavior text does not require real Postgres/network/process semantics; order can be validated with in-process executor spy and temp migration files.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real runner against a temp `migrations/` directory containing out-of-order filenames (e.g., `002_b.sql`, `001_a.sql`, `010_c.sql`) with an in-process DB/executor test double that records execution sequence; assert executed order is `001,002,010`.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test requires module wiring (discovery->ordering->apply), so it would not be a pure Unit test.

behaviors.description: A migration that has already been applied is not re-executed on a subsequent run.
behaviors.justification: BEHAVIOR-QUOTE-GATE: "on a subsequent run" requires persisted state handoff between run 1 and run 2 via `_migrations`. MOCK-MISS: single-function tests miss run-to-run state interaction. REAL-INFRA: can be reproduced with local in-process DB/repo fixture; real OS/subprocess not required by behavior text. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no, (b) no, (c) no, (d) no. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System because behavior does not specifically require validating real network/daemon behavior, only correct stateful orchestration across runs.
behaviors.test_description: Execute runner twice against same test database fixture and same migration files; assert first run applies and records migration, second run performs zero execution calls for already-recorded versions.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Two-run stateful interaction needs Integration; Unit would miss persistence contract.

behaviors.description: When a migration statement fails, its changes are rolled back automatically and no further migrations in the same run are attempted.
behaviors.justification: BEHAVIOR-QUOTE-GATE: "statement fails" + "changes are rolled back automatically" depends on real transaction semantics of PostgreSQL, and MOCK-HIDE risk is high with fake transaction implementations. REAL-INFRA: rollback-on-error is a real DB behavior boundary; real DB process/API call is needed to catch engine/driver interaction failures. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration because in-process fakes can falsely pass rollback behavior that real Postgres/driver transaction handling would fail.
behaviors.test_description: Against a real Postgres instance, run migrations where one file inserts data then triggers SQL error; assert partial writes from that migration are absent after run and later migration files were not executed.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: The test explicitly relies on real Postgres transactional rollback behavior, matching System.

behaviors.description: When two instances of the runner start at the same time against the same database, each migration is applied exactly once across both instances.
behaviors.justification: BEHAVIOR-QUOTE-GATE: "two instances ... start at the same time" and "exactly once across both" is a cross-process concurrency failure boundary. REAL-INFRA: requires real process scheduling/DB locking or constraints; in-process mocks hide race windows. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration because behavior explicitly requires separate concurrent instances, which is subprocess/OS-level behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Launch two runner processes concurrently against the same real Postgres database and identical migration set; assert each migration side effect appears once and `_migrations` has one record per version (no duplicates, no misses).
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Cross-process race detection cannot be reliably validated below System.

behaviors.description: On the very first run against a fresh database, the `_migrations` table is created before any migration file is applied.
behaviors.justification: BEHAVIOR-QUOTE-GATE: "first run against a fresh database" and "created before any migration file is applied" is an initialization-order orchestration check across modules (bootstrap + apply pipeline). REAL-INFRA: no explicit need for subprocess or OS primitives; can be validated with local DB fixture and execution-order instrumentation. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no, (b) no, (c) no, (d) no. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System because behavior text focuses on ordering, not real network/process timing.
behaviors.test_description: Start with empty test DB fixture, run migrator once, and capture executed SQL order; assert `_migrations` DDL occurs before first migration file SQL, and migration records are inserted afterward.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Requires startup sequencing across components; stronger than Unit, but not necessarily real subprocess/System.
